review_summary: The transcript supports two concept questions on CLIP robustness and CoCa, so no extra clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Why can CLIP generalize better than standard ImageNet-trained models on datasets like ObjectNet?
choices: Its text supervision and larger data scale expose it to richer information than category labels alone
It is evaluated only on images it saw during training
It removes all viewpoint variation from the images before training
It uses a nearest-neighbor classifier instead of learned representations
question_bank.explanation: Text paired with images can encode attributes such as shape, color, and context, and large-scale pretraining exposes the model to much broader variation than fixed class labels alone.

question_bank.question: What does CoCa add on top of a CLIP-style contrastive setup?
choices: A decoder with a captioning loss so the model also learns to describe images
A replacement of the image encoder with a pure CNN classifier
A rule that removes all text from training
A segmentation head that predicts masks instead of embeddings
question_bank.explanation: CoCa keeps the contrastive alignment objective but adds caption generation, encouraging the model to learn richer visual information than simple class matching alone.
